chatGPTEstimates.info: Estimated U.S. Average: 29.3 GPT-3 queries per 500 ml (Ren et al. 2023). Note: this takes into account first, second and third-scope water use, including water used in cooling, constructing and making the data center and its components.

Google Cloud Report on Measuring the Environmental Impacts of AI: 0.26 milliliters (or about five drops) of water per Gemini query. Note: this is an estimate for the water used per inference, only taking into account first-scope use for cooling a data center

Altman's own estimate from blog post "The Gentle Singularity" suggests a single query uses 1/15 of a teaspoon of water. Note: this is only the water used for the query itself, and does not take into account the cooling of data center, model training or any further-scope water uses.
